# OAuth2 authentication layer for Memory Core MCP connections

# OAuth2 authentication layer for Memory Core MCP connections

### Context
Per-model GitHub accounts unlock OAuth2-authenticated MCP connections. Each agent connects as an authenticated identity; server resolves caller-identity from OAuth claims rather than trusting client-supplied metadata. Prerequisite for multi-tenant permission enforcement.

### The Problem
MC MCP servers today trust the caller: `add_memory` stores whatever `userId` the client sends; `listMessages` (once #10139 ships) trusts a client-supplied `recipient = me` claim. No spoof protection. Multi-tenant isolation (#10016) is effectively advisory without transport-level auth.

### The Architectural Reality
- Memory Core MCP runs via stdio today; no transport-level auth.
- GitHub supports OAuth2; per-model accounts make each agent a first-class OAuth identity.
- `ai/mcp/server/memory-core/` is the substrate; auth middleware plugs in at server-connect time.
- Related: #10119 surfaces external-agent-facing NL MCP auth — sibling concern, not blocking.

### The Fix
- GitHub OAuth2 flow in Memory Core connect handshake
- Each connection carries AgentIdentity claim resolved from OAuth token → GitHub login → graph AgentIdentity node
- Write operations (`add_memory`, `addMessage`) reject if caller ≠ claimed-author (anti-spoof)
- Read operations scope to caller-identity's tenant; cross-tenant reach requires permission edges (separate sub)
- Local dev / stdio: env-var-pinned identity for single-user mode. Prod requires OAuth.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] OAuth2 handshake handler in MC MCP server
- [ ] Caller-identity resolved and attached to every MCP invocation context
- [ ] Anti-spoof: `add_memory` rejects if caller ≠ claimed-author; test covers legitimate + spoof
- [ ] Dev mode (stdio, pinned identity) documented; does not weaken prod mode
- [ ] Design doc: OAuth identity ↔ AgentIdentity node binding for cross-session stability

### Out of Scope
- OAuth2 for Knowledge Base, GitHub Workflow, Neural Link MCP (separate concerns)
- Cross-tenant permission edges + tests (separate sub)
- #10119 Neo Agent Harness external auth (different substrate)

### Avoided Traps
- **Trust client-supplied userId.** Current behavior; explicitly rejected for prod.
- **OAuth2 across all MCP servers at once.** Scope creep rejected. Memory Core is identity anchor; others adopt incrementally once proven.

### Related
- Parent: #10016
- Sibling: AgentIdentity node sub (blocks this — need identity nodes before auth binds)
- Adjacent: #10119 (different substrate)
- Files: `ai/mcp/server/memory-core/`, OAuth2 handshake middleware (new)
